352	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
353	
354	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
355	
356	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
357	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
358	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
359	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
360	
361	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
362	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
363	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
364	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
365	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
366	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
367	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
368	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
369	
370	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
371	
372	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
373	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
374	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
375	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
376	
377	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
378	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the
379	morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
380	sheafification. -/
381	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
382	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
383	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
384	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
385	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
386	
387	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
388	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
389	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
390	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
391	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
392	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
393	law is definitional. -/
394	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
395	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
396	
397	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
398	
399	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
400	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
401	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
402	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
403	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
404	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
405	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
406	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
407	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
408	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
409	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
410	
411	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
412	
413	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
414	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
415	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
416	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
417	
418	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
419	
420	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
421	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
422	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
423	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
424	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
425	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
426	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
427	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
428	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
429	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
430	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
431	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
432	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
433	
434	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
435	sheaf.**
436	
437	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
438	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
439	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
440	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
441	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
442	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
443	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
444	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
445	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
446	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
447	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
448	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
449	
450	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
451	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
452	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
453	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
454	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
455	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
456	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
457	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
458	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
459	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
460	
461	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
462	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
463	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
464	sheafification counit. -/
465	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
466	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
467	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
468	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
469	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
470	
471	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
472	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
473	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
474	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
475	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
476	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
477	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
478	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
479	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
480	
481	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X` on `⊗`-invertible objects.** (Blueprint
482	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
483	For `M, N, P` `⊗`-invertible (hence locally free of rank one, hence sectionwise
484	flat), there is an isomorphism `(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the
485	objectwise existence-of-iso datum the group law
486	`tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
487	
488	iter-211 status (typed `sorry`, recipe scaffolded): the construction is the
489	three-step composite of the blueprint, absorbing the two nested sheafifications
490	with the flat-whiskering bridge `PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (proven
491	this iter — the go/no-go gate, CLEARED, no `MonoidalClosed`). Writing
492	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`, `η = toSheafify` (the localizer unit), and
493	`α` the presheaf-of-modules associator:
494	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso  (P flat ⇒ right-whiskered `η ∈ J.W`,
495	     by symmetry of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`; `a` inverts `J.W`), giving
496	     `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P = a(a(M.val⊗N.val).val ⊗ P.val) ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
497	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`;
498	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso  (M flat), giving
499	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ a(M.val ⊗ a(N.val⊗P.val).val) = M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
500	The single residual is the bridge `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map
501	f)` (steps 1, 3): provable from `toPresheaf` reflecting isomorphisms + the
502	underlying `AddCommGrpCat`-sheafification localization
503	(`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction` on the underlying
504	adjunction), plus the invertible ⇒ sectionwise-flat derivation feeding
505	`W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`. See task result for the precise residual. -/
506	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
507	    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hN : IsInvertible N) (hP : IsInvertible P) :
508	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
509	  sorry
510	
511	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
512	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
513	
514	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
515	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
516	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
517	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
518	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
519	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
520	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
521	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
522	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
523	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
524	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
525	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
526	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
527	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
528	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
529	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
530	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
531	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
532	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
533	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
534	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
535	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
536	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
537	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
538	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
539	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
540	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
541	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
542	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
543	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
544	
545	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
546	
547	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
548	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
549	
550	This is the single missing-infrastructure ingredient of `A.1.c.SubT`. It says
551	the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor) commutes
552	with the restriction functor `restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f` along an open
553	immersion. Mathematically it factors as: (i) the presheaf-of-modules tensor
554	commutes with the pullback of presheaves (sectionwise this is the
555	extension-of-scalars identity
556	`B ⊗_A (P ⊗_A Q) ≅ (B ⊗_A P) ⊗_B (B ⊗_A Q)` for `A → B`, Stacks 03DM); and
557	(ii) sheafification commutes with pullback along an open immersion (the
558	restriction is exact and the small-Zariski sheafification is local). Neither is
559	in Mathlib at the `SheafOfModules` level (there is no monoidal structure on
560	`SheafOfModules`, hence no strong-monoidal pullback). A genuine construction
561	requires either the `Localization.Monoidal` transport of the monoidal structure
562	through `PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization` together with
563	`W.IsMonoidal` for `W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` (an instance NOT
564	present in Mathlib and the real residual obstacle), or a direct sectionwise
565	construction. Left as a named typed `sorry` feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`;
566	see the task result for the precise missing instance statement. -/
567	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
568	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
569	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
570	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
571	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
572	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
573	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
574	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
575	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
576	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
577	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
578	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
579	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
580	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
581	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
582	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
583	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
584	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
585	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
586	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
587	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
588	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
589	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
590	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
591	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
592	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
593	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
594	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL — CORRECTED iter-208 analysis). The remaining presheaf goal is
595	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
596	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
597	  -- where `φ.hom = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
598	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. The Route-A recipe's claim that this is
599	  -- a "~30–60 LOC sectionwise unfolding of `pullback`" is INCORRECT:
600	  -- `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is the OPAQUE abstract left adjoint
601	  -- `(pushforward φ.hom).leftAdjoint` (`Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`) — it has NO
602	  -- sectionwise formula, and the kaehler precedent (`analogies/...presheafpullback`
603	  -- Decision 5) EXCISED the analogous unfolding. The recipe conflates the opaque
604	  -- `pullback` with the CONCRETE `restrict`/`pushforward` (`restrict_obj` is `rfl`
605	  -- because `M.restrict f = restrictFunctor f = SheafOfModules.pushforward β`, NOT
606	  -- a pullback).
607	  --
608	  -- The genuine route needs TWO project-side ingredients, BOTH absent from Mathlib:
609	  --  (H1, the linchpin) a PRESHEAF-level iso `pushforward β.hom ≅ pullback φ.hom`,
610	  --      where `β.hom` is the open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`
611	  --      (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β.hom).obj M.val` definitionally).
612	  --      Obtain it via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` from a presheaf-level
613	  --      `pushforward β.hom ⊣ pushforward φ.hom` — the presheaf analogue of the
614	  --      sheaf-level `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj`
615	  --      (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`). That analogue needs presheaf-level
616	  --      `pushforwardNatTrans` and `pushforwardCongr` (only the SHEAF versions exist).
617	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β.hom).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
618	  --      (pushforward β.hom).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β.hom).obj B`. Since
619	  --      `pushforward = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars` (`Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:86`),
620	  --      `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is already `Monoidal`, and `β` is along the
621	  --      ring ISO `f.appIso`, this reduces to upgrading the file's lax
622	  --      `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` to STRONG via `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`
623	  --      (`Monoidal/Functor.lean:718`) — needing `IsIso ε` (easy: `ε` is the ring
624	  --      map `f.appIso`, iso) and `IsIso μ`. The `μ`-iso bottoms out in the absent
625	  --      Mathlib lemma "`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring iso is strong
626	  --      monoidal" (`extendScalars` is `Monoidal` but `restrictScalars` is only
627	  --      `LaxMonoidal`, `Monoidal/Adjunction.lean`).
628	  -- Closure once both land:
629	  --   `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`
630	  --      `≅[H1.symm] (pushforward β.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`
631	  --      `≅[H2] (pushforward β.hom M.val) ⊗ₚ (pushforward β.hom N.val)`

633	  -- This is ~200–300 LOC across 4 absent ingredients — `mathlib-build` scale, NOT a
634	  -- single prove-mode close. See `informal/tensorObj_restrict_iso.md` for the full
635	  -- corrected decomposition with exact statements.
636	  sorry
637	
638	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
639	
640	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
641	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
642	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
643	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
644	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
645	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
646	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
647	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
648	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
649	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
650	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
651	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
652	  intro x
653	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
654	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
655	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
656	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
657	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
658	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
659	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
660	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
661	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
662	    tensorObj_unit_iso
663	
664	/-- **Inverse of an invertible module.**
665	
666	Every line bundle `L : X.Modules` has a two-sided tensor inverse: there is a
667	locally-trivial `Linv : X.Modules` (the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)`) together with
668	a tensor isomorphism `L ⊗_X Linv ≅ 𝒪_X`. Per blueprint
669	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`. iter-206 flat-pivot: the designated unit is
670	`SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf = 𝒪_X` (the `MonoidalCategory` unit `𝟙_` is
671	no longer available — the full monoidal instance is off the critical path, see
672	§2). iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body builds the
673	dual and the contraction isomorphism, which is an isomorphism affine-locally on
674	a trivialising cover. -/
675	lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
676	    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
677	    ∃ Linv : X.Modules, LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial Linv ∧
678	      Nonempty (tensorObj L Linv ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
679	  sorry
680	
681	/-- **Restriction of `⊗` to the `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier.**
682	
683	For `S`-schemes `C, T`, the bifunctor `⊗_{C ×_S T}` restricts to the subtype
684	`LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT` of locally-trivial modules on `C ×_S T`, with unit
685	the structure sheaf and the dual as two-sided inverse. Per blueprint
686	`lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: typed `sorry`. -/
687	noncomputable def tensorObjOnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S)
688	    (L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT :=
689	  ⟨tensorObj L.carrier L'.carrier,
690	    tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial L.isLocallyTrivial L'.isLocallyTrivial⟩
691	
692	end Modules
693	
694	/-! ## §4. Consumer: the `AddCommGroup` instance on the relative Picard quotient -/
695	
696	namespace PicSharp
697	
698	/-- **Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient via
699	`Scheme.Modules.tensorObj`.**
700	
701	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, and a test object
702	`πT : T ⟶ S`, the relative Picard quotient
703	`Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) = Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
704	carries a canonical `AddCommGroup` structure with addition the descent of the
705	tensor product `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` of `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj`, neutral
706	element `[O_{C ×_S T}]`, and inverse `-[L] := [L⁻¹]`.
707	
708	Per blueprint `thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS
709	scaffold: typed `sorry`. This is the iter-204+ closure target for the residual
710	`addCommGroup` sorry of `RelPicFunctor.lean` (L235); once this body lands, the
711	RPF instance closes against it. It is supplied as a `def` (rather than a global
712	`instance`) to avoid an instance diamond with the existing typed-`sorry`
713	`PicSharp.addCommGroup` instance in `RelPicFunctor.lean`. -/
714	@[implicit_reducible]
715	noncomputable def addCommGroup_via_tensorObj {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
716	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
717	    AddCommGroup (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :=
718	  sorry
719	
720	end PicSharp
721	
722	end Scheme

# Session 211 — review of iter-211

## Metadata

- **Iteration / session**: 211
- **Sorry count**: 80 → **81** (net **+1**, one new *scaffolded* sorry `tensorObj_assoc_iso`).
- **Build**: GREEN, 0 errors (`lean_diagnostic_messages` clean on the touched file).
- **Axioms**: 0 `axiom` declarations project-wide (blueprint-doctor confirms). The gate
  lemma `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` verified axiom-clean (`propext`, `Classical.choice`,
  `Quot.sound` — **NO `sorryAx`**).
- **File touched**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (only).
- **Model**: opus. Prover status: `done`. `sync_leanok`: iter-211, sha `18c2c89d`, +3 / −0.

## The headline — the go/no-go gate CLEARED; the 5-iter recession pattern broke

This was the **first prover dispatch since iter-208** (iters 209/210 were the sanctioned
no-prover restructure iters: 209 pivoted to ⊗-invertibility, 210 tested+cleared the gate's
buildability and corrected the realization). The iter-211 objective front-loaded the
load-bearing **go/no-go gate** `PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`
(`J.W g → J.W (F ◁ g)` for flat `F`) with a pre-committed reversal: *if it bottoms out in
`MonoidalClosed` / a strong-monoidal pushforward → STOP and ESCALATE to USER.*

**The gate CLEARED, axiom-clean, and the reversal trigger did NOT fire.** The
flat-whiskering route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed` and **no** strong-monoidal pushforward —
realization (2) of `analogies/ts-assoc-gate210.md` is confirmed buildable from present
Mathlib. After 4 iters (205–208) where every "almost there" framing was DISPROVEN on
contact, and 2 deliberate restructure iters, this is the first iter to land genuine,
sorry-free, critical-path infrastructure on the ⊗-group law.

## Targets and attempts

### `PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` — SOLVED (the gate)
- **Approach**: package the two halves (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`,
  `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat`) into `J.W` via
  `GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective`.
- **Code**: `rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢; exact ⟨…hg.1, …hg.2⟩`.
- **Key insight**: the localizer is over `Ab.{u}` (the target of `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf`);
  the instance binder **must** be `Ab.{u}` with the explicit universe or synthesis fails
  (`Ab` defaults to a different universe). `PresheafOfModules.IsLocallySurjective J g` is
  *defeq* to `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J ((toPresheaf _).map g)`, so the helper output
  slots directly into the `W_iff` unfolding.

### `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` — SOLVED (bug fix)
- One half of the gate was **erroring at file open** after a Mathlib bump: the `simp` closing
  `(F.map f.op) a ⊗ₜ 0 = 0` stopped firing because the first tensor factor elaborates in
  `(ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f.op)).obj (F.obj X)`, so `TensorProduct.tmul_zero`'s
  `Module` instance is not synthesizable on the displayed (restrictScalars-wrapped) type.
- **Fix**: `erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl` (erw matches up to the restrictScalars defeq;
  the trailing `0 = 0` needs an explicit `rfl`).
- **Dead ends**: `rw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]`, `exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _`,
  `simp [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]`, `module`, `abel` — all fail on the wrapped module.

### `IsInvertible` — SOLVED (def created)
- `def IsInvertible (M) : Prop := ∃ N, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)`.
- Scheme-level analogue of `Module.Invertible`; carries the inverse existentially so the dual
  is definitional. Resolves the blueprint-reviewer's "IsInvertible does not exist in Lean" concern.

### `tensorObj_left_unitor` / `tensorObj_right_unitor` / `tensorObj_braiding` — SOLVED
- All three via the `sheafification.mapIso (coherence) ≪≫ (asIso counit).app M` pattern.
- **Shared dead-end + fix**: `λ_ M.val` / `ρ_ M.val` / `β_ M.val N.val` FAIL — the
  `MonoidalCategory` / `BraidedCategory` instance lives on
  `PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …)`, but `M.val : PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`
  is not *syntactically* that form, and a type ascription is transparent (does not redirect
  instance resolution). **Force the instance**:
  `(PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val` /
  `BraidedCategory.braiding (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …)) M.val N.val`.
  A `BraidedCategory (PresheafOfModules …)` instance **does** exist in Mathlib.

### `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — PARTIAL (NEW scaffolded sorry, single residual pinned)
- Recipe scaffolded (blueprint 3-step composite). **Single residual = the bridge
  `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f)`** (`a` = PoM sheafification). This is
  **NOT** a Mathlib one-liner — there is no `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`-level
  "map is iso iff underlying in `J.W`" lemma. Must be built from: (a) `toPresheaf` reflecting
  isomorphisms; (b) the underlying `AddCommGrpCat`-sheafification being a localization at `J.W`
  (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction` + a `toPresheaf ∘ sheafification ≅
  AddCommGrp-sheafify ∘ toPresheaf` compatibility); (c) the `IsInvertible ⇒ sectionwise
  Module.Flat` derivation feeding the gate. Plus `W_whiskerRight_of_flat` (cheap — conjugate
  the gate by the braiding). **Est. ~80–150 LOC of sheafification-localization plumbing — a
  focused follow-up, NOT a blocker on the gate.** (Topology `J` is the small-site one of
  `X.ringCatSheaf`, NOT `Scheme.zariskiTopology` — that probe failed.)

### Off-path / downstream — left as typed sorry (per objective)
- `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (off the critical path under the pivot — do NOT re-dispatch),
  `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (off-path), `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (downstream consumer,
  needs the full group-law engine: associator + commMonoid).

## Subagent findings (plan-phase, folded into recommendations)

- **blueprint-reviewer ts211**: HARD GATE CLEARS (`complete: true`, `correct: true`, 0 must-fix).
  Two `soon` findings (both non-blocking): the `IsInvertible` vs `IsLocallyTrivial` carrier
  mismatch (bridge `IsLocallyTrivial → IsInvertible` not blueprinted), and three "stale `\leanok`"
  on sorry decls — see note below. Two `informational`: thin general-M unitor sketch; stale Lean
  module-level docstring.
- **progress-critic ts211**: CONVERGING; pivot GENUINE (the new ingredients do not overlap the
  old `pullback.Monoidal`/`MonoidalClosed` gap); throughput SLIPPING (warned iter-211 must close
  ≥1 sorry). NOTE: the "close ≥1 sorry" bar was NOT met numerically (sorry +1), but the gate
  clearance is the more important signal — see review.md.

## Note on the "stale `\leanok`" finding (resolved — not laundering)

The reviewer flagged `\leanok` on three sorry-bodied decls (`tensorobj_restrict_iso`,
`tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`) as inconsistent and
expected `sync_leanok` to clear them. First-hand audit: these are **statement-block** `\leanok`
markers, which per the marker vocabulary are **legitimate** ("statement block: declaration is
formalized — at least a sorry present"). `sync_leanok` ran at iter-211 (current tree) and
removed 0, confirming they are correct statement-block markers, not proof-block laundering. No
action needed; the reviewer conflated statement-block `\leanok` (legit) with proof-block
`\leanok` (which would be laundering).

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)

- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`: corrected
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat}` →
  `\lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat}` (prover placed the gate in the
  `PresheafOfModules` namespace where its `C/R/J` variables live). This unblocks the
  `\leanok` on this label at the next `sync_leanok` (iter-212) — it could not be added this
  iter because the old pin named a non-existent declaration.

No `\mathlibok` added: all five new decls carry genuine project proofs (not Mathlib
re-exports/aliases), so they are `\leanok`-only (handled by `sync_leanok`). No `\notready`
present to strip.

## Blueprint doctor

No structural findings (no orphan chapters, all `\ref`/`\uses` resolve, no new axioms).

## Recommendations (see recommendations.md)

Close `tensorObj_assoc_iso` via the pinned single residual (build
`isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` + `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`, ~80–150 LOC). Then the
`IsInvertible`-based CommMonoid and `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` consumer. Do NOT re-dispatch
the off-path `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.

# Recommendations for iter-212 (from review of iter-211)

## Headline

iter-211 broke the 5-iter recession pattern: the go/no-go gate
`PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` **CLEARED axiom-clean**, the reversal trigger did
**NOT** fire (no `MonoidalClosed`, no strong-monoidal pushforward), and the ⊗-invertibility
group-law construction is now confirmed buildable. **No USER escalation needed.** The lane is
CONVERGING. Continue it.

## 1. Closest to completion — `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (HIGH priority, single residual pinned)

The associator is a typed sorry whose **single residual is precisely named**: the bridge

```
lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f) → IsIso ((sheafification …).map f)
```

(`J` is the **small-site** topology of `X.ringCatSheaf`, NOT `Scheme.zariskiTopology` — the
zariski probe failed with a type mismatch). Build it from:
- (a) `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf` **reflecting isomorphisms** (a PoM morphism is iso iff its
  underlying additive presheaf map is sectionwise iso);
- (b) the underlying `AddCommGrpCat`-sheafification being a **localization at `J.W`**
  (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction` / `W_iff` /
  `instIsLocalizationFunctorOppositeSheafPresheafToSheafW`), plus a compatibility
  `toPresheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ AddCommGrp-sheafify ∘ toPresheaf`;
- (c) the `IsInvertible ⇒ sectionwise Module.Flat` derivation (invertible ⇒ locally free rank 1
  ⇒ sections flat) that feeds `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`'s `[∀ X, Module.Flat …]` instance.

Plus the cheap `W_whiskerRight_of_flat` (conjugate `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` by the braiding).
**Est. ~80–150 LOC of sheafification-localization plumbing.** This is a focused `prove` (or
`scaffold+prove`) lane, NOT a blocked node. HARD GATE: the chapter cleared `complete+correct`
this iter, so a prover may be dispatched directly (re-confirm via the mandatory blueprint-reviewer
pass next iter, but no writer fix is owed).

## 2. After the associator — the group-law engine (MEDIUM)

- `tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` (`lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`): the prover deliberately did
  NOT scaffold this as a hollow sorry — its carrier type (`Units (Skeleton …)`-shaped iso-classes
  of `IsInvertible` objects) is an undetermined design decision and it *consumes* the associator.
  **Plan agent: pin a precise carrier type mirroring `CommRing.Pic = Units (Skeleton (ModuleCat R))`
  once the associator's bridge lands.** Do not dispatch before the carrier is pinned.
- `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (downstream RPF-L235 consumer): closes only after the associator +
  commMonoid land. Leave parked until then.

## 3. Blueprint / Lean hygiene (LOW, fold into the iter-212 writer/prover directive)

- **`IsInvertible` vs `IsLocallyTrivial` carrier bridge** (blueprint-reviewer `soon` #2): `IsInvertible`
  now EXISTS in Lean (created this iter). But the blueprint's `LineBundle.OnProduct := {M | IsInvertible M}`
  vs the Lean file's `IsLocallyTrivial`-based implementation still needs a one-sentence writer note
  blueprinting the bridge `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial → Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible`. A prover
  connecting `tensorObjOnProduct` to the group-law engine will need this. Dispatch a scoped writer
  on `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` to add it.
- **Thin general-M unitor sketch** (blueprint-reviewer informational): `lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`'s
  sketch covers the self-tensor case better than the general `𝒪_X ⊗ M → M` case; one sentence on
  the counit iso on `𝟙` (relating `O_X.val` to the presheaf unit) would help. The unitors are
  already PROVEN in Lean, so this is doc-only — low priority.
- **Stale Lean module-level docstring** (blueprint-reviewer informational): the docstring still says
  "iter-202 Lane TS file-skeleton" and lists `monoidalCategory` as one of 4 pinned decls
  (removed at the iter-206 pivot). This is a `.lean`-file doc fix the *prover* who next touches the
  file should make (review/plan cannot edit `.lean`). One-line fix.

## 4. Do NOT re-dispatch (off the critical path)

- **`tensorObj_restrict_iso`**: removed from the group-law critical path by the iter-209 pivot. The
  associator uses flat-whiskering, not this restriction-compatibility iso. Its residual is
  ~200–300 LOC across 4 absent Mathlib ingredients (opaque `PresheafOfModules.pullback`; see

- **`exists_tensorObj_inverse`**: also off-path under `IsInvertible` (the inverse is carried by the
  predicate existentially). Leave parked.

## Reusable proof patterns discovered

- **Forcing a monoidal/braided instance through a non-syntactic type**: when `M.val :
  PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` is defeq-but-not-syntactically `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ …)`,
  the `λ_`/`ρ_`/`β_` notation FAILS (type ascription is transparent, does not redirect instance
  resolution). **Force the instance explicitly**:
  `(PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val`,
  `BraidedCategory.braiding (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …)) M.val N.val`.
- **`tmul_zero` under `restrictScalars`**: when a tensor factor is a `restrictScalars`-image module,

  `simp`/`module`/`abel` all fail. Use `erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl`.
- **Localizer universe binder**: the sheafification localizer over presheaves of modules is over
  `Ab.{u}` (target of `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf`); the instance binder must be
  `[J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]` with the **explicit** universe or synthesis fails (`Ab`
  defaults to a different universe).

## Reversal pre-commitment status

The iter-211 reversal trigger (gate bottoming out in `MonoidalClosed`) **did not fire** — it is now
spent. There is no armed escalation entering iter-212. The next genuine reversal would be the
associator residual (`isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`) itself bottoming out in absent multi-file
infra; the prover's diagnosis (toPresheaf reflects isos + AddCommGrp-sheafification is a localization)
judges it present, but iter-212 should watch for it.

The progress-critic ts211 set the bar "iter-211 must close ≥1 sorry to keep within the estimate's
lower bound." Numerically that bar was **not** met: the count went +1, not −1. A mechanical reading

=== Knowledge Base location ===
3:## Overall Progress
222:## Knowledge Base
232:### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
2904:### Known Blockers (do not retry)
3375:## Last Updated
=== total lines ===
3892 .archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md

The lean-vs-blueprint-checker came back clean (0 must-fix, 0 major, 1 cosmetic minor). It surfaced a useful sync note: `lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`'s two-name `\lean{...}` pin didn't receive `\leanok` despite real proofs. Let me read the Knowledge Base section to update it.

222	## Knowledge Base
223	
224	### Soundness Patterns
225	
226	- **Typed-`sorry` on the *carrier of a definition* silently propagates `sorryAx` through every consumer's typeclass synthesis** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical — lean-auditor iter193 finding, dominant project-wide soundness exposure.)* When a `noncomputable def Foo : Carrier := sorry` lands as a file-skeleton placeholder (e.g. `Pic0Scheme`, `PicScheme`, `QuotScheme`, `picSharp`, `divFunctor`, `abelMap`, `PicSharp`, `presheaf`, `PicSharp.etSheaf`), the soundness exposure is qualitatively WORSE than a `theorem foo := sorry`: every typeclass `[Bar (Foo C)]` that fires on `Foo` silently carries `sorryAx`. **Auditor recommendation**: guard the definition behind an existential `Nonempty (Σ' S : Over (Spec k), _)` (or similar) — this keeps the *typeclass-synthesis* honest even when the body is sorry-bodied. The `Σ'`-bundle pattern is already used by other file-skeletons in the project (`jacobianWitness`, etc.). A `refactor` subagent (`pic-quot-relpic-carrier-soundness`) holding write_domain across the 4 affected files (`Picard/{IdentityComponent, FGAPicRepresentability, QuotScheme, RelPicFunctor}.lean`) can atomically re-shape all `:= sorry` definition carriers in one pass without changing downstream consumer signatures (since consumers can be re-shaped to `Classical.choice (nonempty_*)` extractions). Reusable pattern: ANY `noncomputable def F := sorry` on a load-bearing definition deserves the existential-bundle pattern, NOT the bare `:= sorry`.
227	
228	- **Opening an `instance` body's sorry via `refine ⟨...⟩; sorry` *expands* downstream sorryAx propagation rather than contains it** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical — lean-auditor iter195 must-fix on `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:746-772`.)* Iter-195 Lane I advanced the body of `instance instIsRegularInCodimOneProjectiveLineBar` from a bare `:= sorry` to `:= by refine ⟨fun Y => ?_⟩; sorry` (exposing the per-prime-divisor obligation) AND simultaneously advanced the consumer `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` body's tactic chain (Steps A+B+C). The latter's binder list `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]` means the consumer now silently picks up `sorryAx` through more lines of in-scope tactic state than before — the iter is a NET INCREASE in silent propagation surface, not a decrease. **Rule**: when partial structural progress on an instance body is committed (the `refine ⟨...⟩` step), simultaneously demote `instance` → `theorem` and thread `haveI` at consumer sites — otherwise the silent-propagation cost grows alongside the structural progress. Reusable for any iter where Lane progress simultaneously advances a sorry-bodied instance AND its consumer's body. (Companion to iter-193 IdentityComponent.lean entry below; the iter-194 IC.lean fix recipe applies mechanically.)
229	
230	- **`sorry`-derived typeclass instances silently propagate `sorryAx` into every downstream typeclass synthesis automatically** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical — lean-auditor iter193 finding, `IdentityComponent.lean:500-507`.)* A `private instance identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` whose body invokes a sorry-bodied helper `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` (L414-420) is qualitatively worse than a sorry-bodied **theorem** because consumers CANNOT opt out — instance synthesis fires automatically on every `[GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom]` query. **Auditor recommendation**: either (a) demote to a non-instance lemma until the helper closes; or (b) gate the instance behind a separate `[Hypothesis_GC]` typeclass that callers must explicitly supply. Reusable: NEVER mark `instance` on a typeclass derivation whose body transitively invokes `sorryAx` — only `lemma` / `def`.
231	
232	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
233	- **Strip an outer sheafification from an iso goal via `sheafification.mapIso` — reduce a `SheafOfModules`-level iso to a presheaf-level residual** *(NEW iter-208 Archon canonical — Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:356`, one verified reduction step.)* When both sides of an iso goal in `SheafOfModules R` are `(PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).obj _` applied to presheaves of modules (here after `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `sheafificationCompPullback` deposit the LHS as `sheafification.obj ((pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val))` and the RHS is `sheafification.obj ((M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val)` *by definition*), close the outer layer with `refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_` — the residual is the purely presheaf-level comparison, with no sheafification (verify via `lean_goal`). **Reusable** for any "lift a presheaf-of-modules iso through sheafification" reduction. **Caveat**: this exposes — does NOT close — the presheaf residual; if that residual is an `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅ …` base change, you then hit the opaque-abstract-left-adjoint wall (see Known Blockers: the genuine closure is the H1/H2 `pushforward`-bridge route, NOT a sectionwise unfold).

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
- **The ⊗-invertibility group-law gate `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` is PROVEN axiom-clean in Lean — flat-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer with NO `MonoidalClosed`** *(NEW iter-211 Archon canonical — Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:222-340`, 3 axiom-clean decls `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat`, `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`; blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`; the iter-209/210 pivot's go/no-go GATE, CLEARED — reversal trigger did NOT fire.)* For a presheaf of modules `F` sectionwise-flat (`[∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]`) and `g : M ⟶ N` in the sheafification localizer `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)`, the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` is again in `J.W`. **Recipe**: package the two halves via `rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢; exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1, isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩`. Surjectivity half = pure right-exactness (`TensorProduct.induction_on` element-chase, NO flatness). Injectivity half = where flatness enters: `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)` on the kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) → N(X)`, then `TensorProduct.induction_on` on the witness `ζ` with each simple tensor restricting to 0 on a covering sieve (`Presheaf.equalizerSieve` + `hg.equalizerSieve_mem`). **Three reusable idioms (each cost a session somewhere in the window):** (1) **Localizer universe binder MUST be explicit `Ab.{u}`** — the instance binder is `[J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]` (`Ab` = target of `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf`); without the explicit universe, `Ab` defaults to a different universe and synthesis fails. `PresheafOfModules.IsLocallySurjective J g` is *defeq* to `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J ((toPresheaf _).map g)`, so the half-lemmas slot straight into the `W_iff` unfolding. (2) **`tmul_zero` under `restrictScalars`** — when a tensor factor elaborates in `(ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f.op)).obj (F.obj X)`, `TensorProduct.tmul_zero`'s `Module` instance is NOT synthesizable on the displayed (wrapped) type; `rw`/`exact`/`simp`/`module`/`abel` ALL fail. Use `erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl` (erw matches up to the restrictScalars defeq; the trailing `0 = 0` needs the explicit `rfl`). (3) **Forcing a monoidal/braided instance through a non-syntactic type** — `M.val : PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` is defeq-but-not-*syntactically* `PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`, so the `λ_`/`ρ_`/`β_` notation FAILS (type ascription is transparent, does not redirect instance resolution). **Force the instance explicitly:** unitors via `(PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val` / `.rightUnitor M.val`; braiding via `BraidedCategory.braiding (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val` (a `BraidedCategory (PresheafOfModules …)` instance DOES exist in Mathlib). The unitors/braiding/`tensorObj_unit_iso` all close via the same `sheafification.mapIso (coherence) ≪≫ (asIso sheafificationAdjunction.counit).app M` pattern, all axiom-clean. **Reusable** for any "lift a sectionwise-flat / coherence iso through PoM sheafification" need. **Remaining bounded residual** (the associator `tensorObj_assoc_iso`, still a typed sorry): the single gap is the bridge `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f)` (`a` = PoM sheafification) — NOT a Mathlib one-liner; build from `toPresheaf` reflecting isos + the underlying `AddCommGrpCat`-sheafification being a localization at `J.W` (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction`) + the `IsInvertible ⇒ sectionwise Module.Flat` derivation + a cheap `W_whiskerRight_of_flat` (conjugate by the braiding). Est. ~80–150 LOC; the topology `J` is the small-site one of `X.ringCatSheaf`, NOT `Scheme.zariskiTopology`.
